zh.lifeSummary: 當John FitzWalter於1324年出生在英國艾可時，他的父親8 John Pennington24歲，母親Joan (De Multon) Pennington20歲。他於1344年與Alianore Percy在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德阿恩維克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1361年10月18日在英國艾可登莫福享年37歲，埋葬在英國艾可蕾托 登莫福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John FitzWalter于1324年出生在英国艾可时，他的父亲8 John Pennington24岁，母亲Joan (De Multon) Pennington20岁。他于1344年与Alianore Percy在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德阿恩維克结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1361年10月18日在英国艾可登莫福享年37岁，埋葬在英国艾可蕾托 登莫福。

nameMeanings.name: FitzWalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , ‘son of Walter’.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John FitzWalter was born in 1324, in Essex, England, his father, 8 John Pennington, was 24 and his mother, Joan (De Multon) Pennington, was 20. He married Alianore Percy in 1344, in Alnwick, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 18 October 1361, in Dunmow, Essex, England, at the age of 37, and was buried in Little Dunmow, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John FitzWalter est né en 1324, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, 8 John Pennington, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Joan (De Multon) Pennington, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Alianore Percy en 1344, à Alnwick, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 18 octobre 1361, à Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterré à Little Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John FitzWalter 1324 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater 8 John Pennington 24 und seine Mutter Joan (De Multon) Pennington 20. Er hat 1344 in Alnwick, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom Alianore Percy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 18. Oktober 1361 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Dunmow, Essex, England und wurde in Little Dunmow, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John FitzWalter è nato il nel 1324 nel Essex, England, il suo padre, 8 John Pennington, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Joan (De Multon) Pennington, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Alianore Percy nel 1344, nel Alnwick, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 18 ottobre 1361, nel Dunmow, Essex, England, all'età di 37 anni, e fu sepolto a Little Dunmow, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John FitzWalterが1324年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父8 John Penningtonは24歳，母Joan (De Multon) Penningtonは20歳であった。1344年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドアルンウィックでAlianore Percyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1361年10月18日にイギリスエセックスダンモウで37歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスリットル ダンモウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John FitzWalter(이)가 1324년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 8 John Pennington(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan (De Multon) Pennington(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1344년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 알윅에서 Alianore Percy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1361년 10월 18일 영국 에세스 던모에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 리틀 던모에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John FitzWalter nasceu em 1324, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, 8 John Pennington, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Joan (De Multon) Pennington, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Alianore Percy em 1344, em Alnwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 18 de outubro de 1361, em Dunmow, Essex, Inglaterra, com 37 anos, e foi sepultado em Little Dunmow, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John FitzWalter родился в 1324 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени 8 John Pennington было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Joan (De Multon) Pennington, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Alianore Percy в 1344 г. в местности алнвикк, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 18 октября 1361 г. в местности дунмоу, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 37 лет и был похоронен в местности литл дунмоу, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John FitzWalter nació en 1324, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, 8 John Pennington, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Joan (De Multon) Pennington, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Alianore Percy en 1344, en Alnwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 18 de octubre de 1361, en Dunmow, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 37 años, y fue sepultado en Little Dunmow, Essex, Inglaterra.
